briefdescription.para: No symbols required.

detaileddescription.para: Only symbols taken from symbol tables are provided. On Windows, these are the export symbols. On Linux, these are the symbols from .dynsym and .symtab sections.

briefdescription.para: Debug symbols (Currently implemented as DEBUG_AND_EXPORT_SYMBOLS)

detaileddescription.para: IFUNC symbols (Linux only). When this flag is specified, Pin resolves IFUNC symbols to their actual implementations and provides those as symbols. This flag can be combined with EXPORT_SYMBOLS and/or DEBUG_SYMBOLS. Specifying only this flag has the same effect as NO_SYMBOLS.
Export (symbol table) symbols are provided along with debug symbols if they are available and not explicitly disabled. This is the default mode on both Windows & Linux.
List of supported modes of symbolic information delivery. Used in PIN_InitSymbolsAlt().
Type of function to be called when an image is loaded
Type of function to be called before processing symbols of an image. If the function returns FALSE, the symbol processing is skipped for the image.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *filename, ADDRINT start, USIZE size, ADDRINT loadOffset, BOOL mainExecutable)
memberdef.name: IMG_CreateAt

briefdescription.para: Returns a IMG object for image loaded outside of PIN's loader.

detaileddescription.para: Static model for instrumenting IMG's once they are loaded into memory.
Tool should use this IMG object to mark the routines inside it, using RTN_CreateAt(). After all the routines are marked, tool should call IMG_ReplayImageLoad() to finalize the image load procedure.

parameterdescription.para: name of the image visible to Pin tools
memory address where first byte of the image is loaded to
the size of the image in memory. This means that the image region in memory will be at [start .. start+size)
the offset between the memory addresses specified in the executable/library files and the memory addresses where this image was actaully loaded
TRUE if this is the main image

simplesect.para: IMG object that represents the image. Please make sure to call IMG_ReplayImageLoad() on the IMG object after you're done creating all the routines in the IMG object.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: All CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Replays the image load of an IMG object (created by IMG_CreateAt()).

detaileddescription.para: This means that all the image load callbacks will be called for the specified IMG object. If PIN_StartProgram() wasn't called yet, PIN will enqueue this request and will process this request after PIN_StartProgram() was called

parameterdescription.para: The image object to replay its load

simplesect.para: The vm and pin client locks are obtained during the call of this API.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: All CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Gets a read-only copy of loader information for the image which is used in PIN ADX debugger.

parameterdescription.para: The image object to act on

simplesect.para: Pointer to OS specific structure that holds data about loader information. On Linux, this is a pointer to struct LINUX_LOADER_IMAGE_INFO.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: Linux CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Sets the loader information for the image which are used in PIN ADX debugger.

parameterdescription.para: The image object to act on
Points to OS specific structure that holds data about loader information. On Linux, this is a pointer to struct LINUX_LOADER_IMAGE_INFO.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: Linux CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the image loaded after the given image, or IMG_Invalid() if it is the last image.

detaileddescription.para: Of the list of currently loaded images in memory it returns the image loaded after image img, or IMG_Invalid() if img is the last image
Skip shadow image (vdso or dynamic code)

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the image loaded prior to the given image, or IMG_Invalid() if it is the first image.

detaileddescription.para: Of the list of currently loaded images in memory it returns the image loaded prior to image img, or IMG_Invalid() if img is the first image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns an invalid image value used to indicate no image.

simplesect.para: Used to indicate no image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the image is valid.

simplesect.para: True if img is not IMG_Invalid()

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the first section in the image.

simplesect.para: First section in image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the last section in the image.

simplesect.para: Last section in image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the first regular symbol in the image.

simplesect.para: First regular symbol in image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of first instruction executed when image is loaded.

simplesect.para: Address of first instruction executed when image is loaded
DEPRECATED: API returned wrong value in case of position independent code. Use IMG_EntryAddress() instead.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of first instruction executed when image is loaded.

simplesect.para: Address of first instruction executed when image is loaded

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if image has the specified property.

parameterdescription.para: the property to check of type IMG_PROPERTY

simplesect.para: if image supports specific property in specific mode

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the fully qualified actual file name of the image.

detaileddescription.para: Image names are encoded in UTF8 (a superset of ASCII), this is supported for Linux (only for locales encoded in UTF8) and Windows

simplesect.para: Fully qualified actual file name of image.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the global pointer (GP) of the image, if a GP is used to address global data.

simplesect.para: Global pointer (GP) of image, if a GP is used to address global data

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the offset from the image's link-time address to its load-time address.

simplesect.para: Offset from the image's link-time address to its load-time address.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Tells the lowest address of any code or data loaded by the image.

simplesect.para: If the image is split in memory, regions from other images might be mapped between its regions. In this case, the function will return the low address of the text segment. See IMG_NumRegions.
The image's lowest address or the text segment low address if the image is split.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Tells the highest address of any code or data loaded by the image.

detaileddescription.para: This is the address of the last byte loaded by the image.

simplesect.para: If the image is split in memory, regions from other images might be mapped between its regions. In this case, the function will return the high address of the text segment. See IMG_NumRegions.
The image's highest address or the text segment high address if the image is split.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the start of the raw image file.

detaileddescription.para: On Windows, the whole image has been loaded by system loader at once. IMG_StartAddress() gives the pointer to the image, mapped by loader. If you are working inside image-load callback - IMG_LowAddress() and IMG_StartAddress() return the same value.

On Unix, the loader maps only portions of the image file that contain code and data. Additionally, Pin maps the whole image file for parsing. IMG_StartAddress() returns a pointer to the memory mapped file. After return form image-load callback the whole image file is being unmapped and the pointer becomes invalid. The IMG_StartAddress() returns 0.
Note, On Unix, the IMG_LowAddress() and IMG_StartAddress() return different values.
On IMG_Open(), Pin maps the whole image into memory and the pointer is valid until IMG_Close()

simplesect.para: Pointer to the start of the raw image file.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Tells the size of the raw image mapped by Pin.

detaileddescription.para: Tells the size of the raw image mapped by Pin, including the size of the image's symbolic information, which is not normally mapped by the application. Use this with IMG_StartAddress() to find the entire memory range of the raw image mapped by Pin.
Note, this does not give the address range of the image from the application's perspective. To get that, use IMG_LowAddress() and IMG_HighAddress().
On Linux, the file is mapped in image-load callback or after IMG_Open. In other cases the IMG_SizeMapped returns 0.

simplesect.para: Size (bytes) of the raw image file mapped by Pin.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the image type.

simplesect.para: Image type

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if this is the main executable image.

simplesect.para: TRUE for the image Pin was applied on in the command line (i.e. first param after )

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the image has debug information that includes the lines information.

simplesect.para: TRUE if img has debug information that includes the lines information.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if this is the image of the application's loader (interpreter).

detaileddescription.para: On operating systems that have the concept of loader image. (i.e. another program that is resposible to load the application), this function return TRUE for the image of the loader. In operating system that don't have the concept of loader, this function returns FALSE on all images.

simplesect.para: TRUE if this is the image of the application's loader (interpreter).

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if this is the static executable.

simplesect.para: TRUE if this is the static executable

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if this is the VDSO image.

detaileddescription.para: On Linux this function return TRUE for the image of the VDSO. On other operating systems return FALSE for all images.

simplesect.para: TRUE if this is the VDSO image.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the number of consecutive regions of the image in memory.

simplesect.para: number of consecutive regions of the image in memory

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the high address of the n'th region.

simplesect.para: n starts at 0
the high address of the n'th region

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the low address of the n'th region.

simplesect.para: n starts at 0
the low address of the n'th region

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the image that contains this section.

simplesect.para: Image that contains this section

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the section that follows the given section, or SEC_Invalid() if it is last.

simplesect.para: Section that follows sec, or SEC_Invalid() is sec is last section in the image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the previous section, or SEC_Invalid() if this is the first in the image.

simplesect.para: Previous section of sec, or SEC_Invalid() if sec is the first in the image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns an invalid section value.

simplesect.para: Invalid section value

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the section is valid.

simplesect.para: True if sec is not SEC_Invalid()

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the first RTN of the section, or RTN_Invalid() if no RTNs.

simplesect.para: First RTN of sec, or RTN_Invalid() if no RTNs

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the last RTN of the section, or RTN_Invalid() if no RTNs.

simplesect.para: Last RTN of sec, or RTN_Invalid() if no RTNs

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the section name.

simplesect.para: Section name

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the section type.

simplesect.para: Section type

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if section is mapped in memory.

simplesect.para: True if section is mapped in memory, unmapped section contain data not needed at run time, like debug information

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the raw data for the section.

detaileddescription.para: On Linux, Pin maps the whole image file for processing. The pointer returned by SEC_Data() points inside the mapped file. Note, SEC_Data() pointer is always between IMG_StartAddress() and IMG_StartAddress() + IMG_SizeMapped(). If you are analyzing image in image-load callback, please remember that the image will be unmapped after the callback returns and the pointer provided by SEC_Data() becomes invalid. If you are working with IMG_Open() the pointer is valid until IMG_Close().
If section does not have raw data the function returns 0.

simplesect.para: Return a pointer to the raw data for the section

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the section that contains this routine.

simplesect.para: Section that contains this routine

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the routine that follows this routine, or RTN_Invalid() if it is the last.

simplesect.para: Routine that follows rtn, or RTN_Invalid() if rtn is the last in the section

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the routine that precedes this routine, or RTN_Invalid() if it is the first.

simplesect.para: Routine that precedes rtn, or RTN_Invalid() if rtn is the first in the section

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns an RTN value that indicates no valid image.

simplesect.para: RTN value that indicates no valid image

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the RTN is valid.

detaileddescription.para: RTN_Valid() returns FALSE in certain cases when there is no static image of the code available, including dynamically generated code.

simplesect.para: True if rtn is not RTN_Invalid().

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the name of the routine.

simplesect.para: Name of routine

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the RTN is artificial.

detaileddescription.para: An artificial RTN is an RTN which was introduced by PIN for internal management and does not really represent an actual routine in the application. For example, PIN might cover code pieces that are not associated with symbols with artificial RTNs in order to meet the requirement that all code must be covered with RTNs.

simplesect.para: TRUE if RTN is artificial.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the SYM associated with the given routine.

simplesect.para: SYM associated with the given routine

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the method ID associated with the given routine (JIT Profiling).

simplesect.para: Method ID associated with the given routine (JIT Profiling)

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: Linux, Windows & MacOS CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Converts an RTN to a function pointer.

simplesect.para: function pointer

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns a unique ID for the routine.

detaileddescription.para: Pin assigns each routine a unique ID. The ID is globally unique, i.e. an ID will not appear in two images. If the same routine name exists in two different images (i.e. they are in different addresses), each will have a different ID. If an image is unloaded and then reloaded, the routines within it will most likely have different IDs than before.

simplesect.para: Unique ID for the routine.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the first instruction of the BBL.

simplesect.para: First instruction of bbl

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the last instruction of the BBL.

simplesect.para: Last instruction of bbl

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the next BBL or BBL_INVALID() if this is the end of trace or rtn.

simplesect.para: Next bbl or BBL_INVALID() if this is the end of trace or rtn

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the previous BBL or BBL_INVALID() if this is the beginning of trace or rtn.

simplesect.para: Previous bbl or BBL_INVALID() if this is the beginning of trace or rtn

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the BBL is valid.

simplesect.para: True if bbl is not BBL_INVALID()

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the routine that contains this instruction.

detaileddescription.para: See RTN_FindByAddress for more information.

simplesect.para: Routine that contains this instruction

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the instruction that follows this instruction.

simplesect.para: Instruction that follows ins, or INS_Invalid() if ins is the last in the rtn or trace

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the instruction that precedes this instruction.

simplesect.para: Instruction that precedes ins, or INS_Invalid() if ins is the first in the rtn or trace

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns an invalid instruction used in iterators.

simplesect.para: Invalid instruction used in iterators INS_Prev and INS_Next

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the instruction is valid.

simplesect.para: TRUE if ins is not INS_Invalid()

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of the instruction.

simplesect.para: Address of instruction

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the size of instruction in bytes.

simplesect.para: Size of instruction in bytes

briefdescription.para: Returns the target address for direct branches or calls.

simplesect.para: For direct branches or calls, the target address
DEPRECATED: see INS_DirectControlFlowTargetAddress() instead.

briefdescription.para: Returns the target address for direct control-flow instructions.

detaileddescription.para: Must verify that INS_IsDirectControlFlow() is true before using this function. Refer to INS_IsControlFlow() for more details on control-flow instructions.

simplesect.para: the target address for direct control-flow instructions.

briefdescription.para: Gets the address of the next instruction.

simplesect.para: Address of instruction that follows this one

briefdescription.para: Returns the routine that follows this symbol, or SYM_Invalid() if it is the last.

simplesect.para: Routine that follows sym, or SYM_Invalid() if sym is the last in the section

briefdescription.para: Returns the routine that precedes this symbol, or SYM_Invalid() if it is the first.

simplesect.para: Routine that precedes sym, or SYM_Invalid() if sym is the first in the section

briefdescription.para: Returns the name of the symbol.

simplesect.para: Name of symbol

briefdescription.para: Returns a value used to indicate no symbol.

simplesect.para: Used to indicate no symbol

briefdescription.para: Checks if the symbol is valid.

simplesect.para: True if sym is not SYM_Invalid()

briefdescription.para: Checks if the symbol is a dynamic symbol.

simplesect.para: True if sym is a dynamic symbol

briefdescription.para: Checks if the symbol was generated by Pin.

simplesect.para: True if sym is a symbol which did not appear in the image's original symbol table and was added by Pin (for example when resolving an ifunc). False if the symbol existed in the image's symbol table

briefdescription.para: Checks if the symbol is an IFUNC implementation symbol.

simplesect.para: True if sym is an IFUNC implementation symbol

briefdescription.para: Checks if the symbol is an IFUNC resolver symbol.

simplesect.para: True if sym is an IFUNC resolver symbol

briefdescription.para: Returns the value of the symbol, usually an address relative to beginning of image.

simplesect.para: Value of symbol, usually an address relative to beginning of image

briefdescription.para: Returns the section index of a symbol.

detaileddescription.para:
sym
The symbol object
Pin keeps the symbols ordered by their address location in the image. The index represents the order in which they were found when processing the different symbol table sections and debug information.

simplesect.para: There is no guarantee that the index represents the order of the symbols in the image. Also, when both symbol table and debug information are available, all that can be said is that the symbol table symbols will have lower indexes than the debug information symbols.
section index of a symbol

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of the symbol in memory.

simplesect.para: address of the symbol in memory

briefdescription.para: Returns a unique ID for the image.

detaileddescription.para: If an image is unloaded, the ID is not reused for a different image. If an image is unloaded and the same one is loaded back, the ID is different.

simplesect.para: Unique ID for the image.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the pointer to the object that was passed by the application for reporting the creation of a dynamic image via the Jit Profiling API.

detaileddescription.para: The pointer is available only for dynamic images, and only during the image load and routine instrumentation callbacks. In all other cases, the function returns NULL.

simplesect.para: pointer to the object, or NULL.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: Linux* CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Finds an image by its ID.

parameterdescription.para: The image ID to search for

simplesect.para: IMG object, valid or invalid

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Finds an image by address.

detaileddescription.para: For each image, check if the address is within the mapped memory region of one of its segments.

simplesect.para: IMG object, valid or invalid
The pin client lock must be obtained before calling this API from outside an instrumentation callback.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Adds a function used to instrument at routine granularity.

parameterdescription.para: Passed as the second argument to the instrumentation function

simplesect.para: PIN_CALLBACK A handle to a callback that can be used to further modify this callback's properties

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Registers a callback to catch the loading of an image.

parameterdescription.para: Instrumentation function for images, it is passed an image and v
the value of to pass to fun when an image is loaded

simplesect.para: PIN_CALLBACK A handle to a callback that can be used to further modify this callback's properties
The pin client lock must be obtained before calling this API from outside main() or instrumentation callbacks.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Registers a callback to be used when an image is unloaded.

detaileddescription.para: This is not an instrumentation functionit doesn't make sense to instrument a function when it removed from memory.

parameterdescription.para: passed an image and v when an image is unloaded
the value to pass to fun when an image is unloaded

simplesect.para: PIN_CALLBACK A handle to a callback that can be used to further modify this callback's properties
The pin client lock must be obtained before calling this API from outside main() or instrumentation callbacks.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Registers a callback to be invoked before processing debug information for the image.

detaileddescription.para: If the callback returns FALSE, debug information processing is skipped for the image. This API can be used to optimize symbol (SYM) processing when the tool is interested only in symbols (SYM/RTN) from certain images. In some cases, skipping debug information processing can significantly reduce Pin's initialization time or image load processing time.

By the time the callback is called, the image is already loaded and most image information is available. However, RTNs are not yet created, and only symbols (SYM) from the export/symbol table are available. It is recommended not to emit any instrumentation from within this callback; use it only to decide whether to process debug information symbols for the image.

parameterdescription.para: passed an image and v before processing symbols from the image's debug information
the value to pass to fun when it is called

simplesect.para: Symbols (SYM) from the export/symbol table are always processed, regardless of the return value of this callback.
Skipping debug information processing only affects RTN construction and SYM APIs. It does not affect PIN_GetSourceLocation API.
The pin client lock must be obtained before calling this API from outside main() or instrumentation callbacks.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the range of routine in bytes.

detaileddescription.para: (until the next known symbol or end of current code region).

simplesect.para: range of routine in bytes

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the size of routine in bytes.

simplesect.para: size of routine in bytes

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the resolver function that led to this implementation (ifunc).

simplesect.para: The resolver function that led to this implementation (ifunc)

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the implementation function that this ifunc points to.

simplesect.para: The implementation function that this ifunc points to

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Allows one to open an image and browse it statically.

detaileddescription.para: There can only be one image open at a time. File names are encoded in UTF8 (a superset of ASCII).

simplesect.para: This API can only be used at a safe point, which is before calling PIN_StartProgram()(/or similar)
The opened image, or IMG_INVALID() if the image cannot be opened.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Closes the open image.

parameterdescription.para: The IMG that was previously opened.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the name of a routine containing the given address.

detaileddescription.para: If more than one name is associated with this address, the first name found is returned.
See RTN_FindByAddress for more information.

parameterdescription.para: Memory address that corresponds to the RTN

simplesect.para: Name of routine, or "" if it is not found
The pin client lock is obtained during the call of this API.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Finds the routine that contains the given memory address.

parameterdescription.para: Memory address that corresponds to an RTN

simplesect.para: Handle to the RTN found. If not found returns RTN_Invalid()

If a valid RTN handle is returned then it means that the given address was within the RTN range. To verify that the given address is the start of the RTN, you can compare it with the address returned by RTN_Address.

In a multithreaded program, the returned RTN handle could go stale if another thread unloads the shared object that contains the RTN. Use PIN_LockClient() before calling this routine and PIN_UnlockClient() after the last use of the returned RTN handle. Locking is automatic from an instrumentation routine, so it is unnecessary (but harmless) to lock calls to this function from an instrumentation routine. If you just want the name, call RTN_FindNameByAddress, which automatically does the locking and returns a string which will not go stale if the shared library is unloaded
The pin client lock must be obtained before calling this API from outside an instrumentation callback.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Finds a routine in an image by name.

detaileddescription.para: This API will also go over mangled names and demangle them for comparison.

parameterdescription.para: Image in which to search for RTN
Name of the RTN to search in IMG

simplesect.para: Handle to the RTN found. If not found returns RTN_Invalid()
If more than one RTN matches the name, the first one found will be returned. This can happen for overloaded functions. To find the exact overload, the correct mangled name should be passed.

Because this function will iterate over all RTNs in the image, and will demangle each name if the given name is not an exact match, it may have severe performance implications. For finding several RTNs per image or for matching all overloads, it is recommended that the caller iterates over the RTNs manually and decides on the search criteria themselves. See PIN_UndecorateSymbolName for information about name demangling.
This function internally uses SYM APIs. So PIN_InitSymbols should be called for this API to work.
The pin client lock must be obtained before calling this API from outside an instrumentation callback.

In case this function is an Ifunc the return value will be the RTN that holds the implementation of that ifunc (Notice! this RTN can be on another image. If the resolver function is needed use RTN_IFuncResolver().

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Opens the given RTN.

detaileddescription.para: This must be called before RTN_InsHead() or RTN_InsertCall() or RTN_InsHeadOnly()

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Closes the given RTN.

detaileddescription.para: This must be called before opening a new RTN.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the first instruction of the RTN, or INS_Invalid() if no instructions.

detaileddescription.para: You must call RTN_Open() before the first time this is called for an RTN.

Note that Pin find the INSs of the RTN through static discovery, so Pin does not guarantee that it will find all the INSs in the RTN. If you need completely reliable instructions, use normal JIT time instrumentation, where Pin can guarantee that the instructions are correct.

simplesect.para: RTN_InsHeadOnly(), which is provided for performance purposes. If a tool wishes to examine only the first INS of an RTN: Routine Object it should use RTN_InsHeadOnly() instead of RTN_InsHead().
First instruction of rtn, or INS_Invalid() if no instructions.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the first instruction of the RTN, or INS_Invalid() if no instructions.

detaileddescription.para: You must call RTN_Open() before the first time this is called for an RTN.

Note that tools should use this function when they want to examine ONLY the first INS of an RTN, and NO others. The INS_Next() of the INS returned by this function may be INS_Invalid() even if there are more INSs in the RTN. Tools that want to examine further INSs of the RTN should call the RTN_InsHead() function instead of this one.

simplesect.para: First instruction of rtn, or INS_Invalid() if no instructions.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the last instruction of the RTN, or INS_Invalid() if no instructions.

detaileddescription.para: You must call RTN_Open() before the first time this is called for an RTN.

Note that Pin finds the INSs of the RTN through static discovery, so Pin does not guarantee that it will find all the INSs in the RTN. If you need completely reliable instructions, use normal JIT time instrumentation, where Pin can guarantee that the instructions are correct.

simplesect.para: Last instruction of rtn, or INS_Invalid() if no instructions

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Computes the number of static INSs inside the RTN.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Inserts a call relative to an RTN.

parameterdescription.para: Use IPOINT_BEFORE to call funptr before execution, or IPOINT_AFTER for immediately before the return
IARG_TYPE. Arguments to pass to funptr

simplesect.para: IPOINT_AFTER is implemented by instrumenting each return instruction in a routine. Pin tries to find all return instructions, but success is not guaranteed
Pin does not support calling this function from either the TRACE or INS InstrumentationFunction callback

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the address in memory of the RTN.

simplesect.para: Address in memory of rtn

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Creates a routine object at given address.

detaileddescription.para: In some situations user can calculate address of routine, but Pin doesn't see it because there is no symbol at this point. RTN_CreateAt() allows user to create a routine at a given address and assign a name to it. When it is done, the routine can be searched for by address or by name. The information is kept in Pin as long as the containing image is in memory.

The address should point to code (an executable section or segment). Since the whole code is "covered" by routine objects, the address should fall in one of the existing routines. Pin shortens the routine, which contains the given address, and creates a new routine which starts at the given address and continues till the next routine or the end of the code section. Close any open routine before calling this interface with RTN_Close().

parameterdescription.para: The start address of the new routine
The assigned name of the new routine
True if the symbol's type which corresponds to the new routine location is ifunc symbol.

simplesect.para: RTN object The routine object is valid if the address fails into code section.
The pin client lock is obtained during the call of this API.
If there is another routine object which starts at the same address this function replaces its name.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Checks if the routine is dynamically created.

detaileddescription.para: A routine can be marked as dynamically created using Jit Profiling API only.

simplesect.para: TRUE if the routine is dynamically created

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the address in memory of the section.

detaileddescription.para: If image is loaded by system loader, the real virtual address of section will be returned. If the image is mapped by IMG_Open() the address will be inside the mapped image. On Unix, if the section is not loadable, 0 will be returned in the both cases.

simplesect.para: Address in memory of sec

briefdescription.para: Checks if the section is readable.

simplesect.para: TRUE if section is readable

briefdescription.para: Checks if the section is writable.

simplesect.para: TRUE if section is writable

briefdescription.para: Checks if the section is executable.

simplesect.para: TRUE if section is executable

briefdescription.para: Returns the size of the section.

simplesect.para: Size of section

briefdescription.para: Checks if the BBL data structure has been instrumented or optimized since it was created.

simplesect.para: Whether the BBL data structure has been instrumented or optimized since it was created

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of a BBL.

simplesect.para: Address of a bbl

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the size of the BBL code in bytes.

simplesect.para: Size of bbl code, in bytes

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the first image loaded into memory.

simplesect.para: The first image loaded into memory

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Returns the last image loaded into memory.

simplesect.para: The last image loaded into memory

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Linux, Windows CPU: All

briefdescription.para: Undecorates symbol name.

detaileddescription.para: Provides undecoration of C++ names and decorated C names. The behavior of this function is compiler and OS specific. The C++ mangled name is undecorated either to its full signature (UNDECORATION_COMPLETE style) or to [scope::]name form (UNDECORATION_NAME_ONLY style). Example of undecoration of symbol generated by Microsoft compiler in Windows: StyleOriginalnameUndecoratedname ============================================================================= UNDECORATION_COMPLETE?foo@ccc@@QAEPADH@Zpublic:char*__thiscallccc::foo(int) UNDECORATION_NAME_ONLYccc::foo
Symbol name decorated according to Windows IA32 C calling conventions is undecorated as follows: _foo->foo(__cdeclconvention) _foo@4->foo(__stdcallconvention) @foo@12->foo(__fastcallconvention)

Example of undecoration of symbol generated by GCC compiler on Linux: StyleOriginalnameUndecoratedname ============================================================================= UNDECORATION_COMPLETE_ZN1A7method1ERKSt6vectorIiSaIiEEA::method1(std::vector<int,std::allocator<int>>const&) UNDECORATION_NAME_ONLYA::method1
Undecoration of macOS* symbols is done similarly to Linux (Remove the leading '_' and undecorate like Linux)

You should be careful if using this function, since some of the undecorated names it generates for symbols generated by the compiler may not be valid C++ symbol names. For example on Linux demangling can give names like construction vtable for std::istream-in-std::iostream, or non-virtual thunk to std::strstream::~strstream(), which are not valid C++ names.

parameterdescription.para: Undecoration style, relevant only to C++ undecoration Values: UNDECORATION_COMPLETE undecorate to full signature UNDECORATION_NAME_ONLY undecorate to [scope::]name

simplesect.para: string containing undecorated symbol name. If undecoration fails or not supported, the function returns the unmodified original name.

simplesect.title: Availability:
simplesect.para: Mode: JIT & Probe O/S: Windows, Linux CPU: All
